name: TensorDock (Affordable GPU Cloud Marketplace)
pricing_notes: Usage-based; community GPU marketplace; A100/H100/RTX4090 from $0.40/hr

auth.notes: TensorDock API key for programmatic deployment

performance.notes: VM deployment takes minutes; GPU performance equals hardware spec

agent_readiness.notes: Community GPU cloud marketplace. Raw GPU VMs (not managed). Deploy: POST /api/v0/gpu/deploy. List available: GET /api/v0/gpu/hostnodes. GPU types: A100 80GB, H100, RTX 4090, RTX 3090, A40. SSH + Jupyter access. Pay per hour. No commitment. Spot-like pricing. Cheaper than Lambda Labs for hobbyist GPU access. Docker preinstall option. Less reliable than enterprise clouds but very affordable.

gotchas: Community nodes may have availability issues
Less reliable than enterprise GPU clouds
Raw VMs require self-managed software setup
Nodes can be taken offline by node operators
best_when: Hobbyist or researcher needing cheap GPU access
Short training runs where interruption acceptable
Testing GPU code before scaling on expensive clouds
avoid_when: Production ML training requiring reliability SLAs
summary: TensorDock is a community GPU marketplace with very affordable pricing (A100 from ~$0.40/hr, RTX 4090 ~$0.20/hr). Raw GPU VMs via REST API or web UI. SSH + Jupyter access. Docker preinstall. Best for budget ML experiments where reliability SLAs aren't critical. Alternative to Vast.ai and RunPod for cheap community GPU access.
